title: Tool Use & Function Calling
description: Developer equips agents with callable tools; the AI provider requests tool invocations, results are fed back into the conversation.

actors.name: Developer
actors.description: Creates custom tools, registers them on agents, configures provider tools

actors.name: Agent
actors.description: AnonymousAgent implements Agent, Conversational, HasTools — exposes tools() iterable to the runtime

actors.name: AI Provider
actors.description: Receives serialised tool schemas, decides when to invoke a tool, returns tool_call requests

actors.name: Gateway
actors.description: Translates between domain objects and provider-specific HTTP API; uses MapsTools + InvokesTools traits

use_cases.name: Scaffold a new Tool class
use_cases.description: Run `php artisan make:tool ToolName` (MakeToolCommand extends GeneratorCommand). Generates stub in App\Ai\Tools namespace with Tool interface contract.
use_cases.notes: Stub path: stubs/tool.stub. --force flag overwrites existing.

use_cases.name: Implement Tool contract
use_cases.description: Implement Tool interface: description():string, schema(JsonSchema):array<string,Type>, handle(Request):string. Schema drives the JSON function definition sent to the AI provider.
use_cases.notes: Tool::handle() receives Tools\Request (wraps arguments array, implements ArrayAccess + InteractsWithData).

use_cases.name: Register Tools on an Agent
use_cases.description: Implement HasTools::tools():iterable on an Agent class (or use AnonymousAgent). Return mix of Tool and ProviderTool instances.
use_cases.notes: AnonymousAgent constructor accepts (instructions, messages, tools). StructuredAnonymousAgent adds structured output.

use_cases.name: Use a Provider-Native Tool
use_cases.description: Return ProviderTool subclasses (WebSearch, WebFetch, FileSearch) from tools(). These bypass the user-land handle() cycle and are executed by the AI provider itself.
use_cases.notes: WebSearch: maxSearches, allowedDomains, location(city,region,country). Provider must implement SupportsWebSearch / SupportsFileSearch.

use_cases.name: Map Tools to Provider API Format
use_cases.description: MapsTools trait iterates tools(): ProviderTool instances → mapProviderTool() (type:web_search_preview / file_search); Tool instances → mapTool() builds JSON function definition {type,name,description,strict,parameters}.
use_cases.notes: Tool name = class_basename($tool). Schema flattened via ObjectSchema::toSchema() using Illuminate JsonSchema.

use_cases.name: Invoke Agent Prompt (with Tools)
use_cases.description: GeneratesText::prompt(AgentPrompt) builds messages list, calls listenForToolInvocations(), then calls textGateway()->generateText(..., tools:$agent->tools(), ...). Returns AgentResponse with toolCalls + toolResults.
use_cases.notes: Middleware pipeline applied first (RememberConversation, custom). Events: PromptingAgent → AgentPrompted.

use_cases.name: Execute Tool on Provider Request
use_cases.description: Gateway parses provider response for tool_call entries. Calls InvokesTools::findTool(name, tools) then executeTool(tool, arguments): fires invokingToolCallback → tool.handle(new Request(arguments)) → fires toolInvokedCallback → returns string result.
use_cases.notes: findTool() matches by class_basename. Result cast to string and returned to provider as tool_result message.

use_cases.name: Observe Tool Invocation Events
use_cases.description: Listen to InvokingTool (before) and ToolInvoked (after) Laravel events. Both carry invocationId, toolInvocationId, agent, tool, arguments; ToolInvoked also carries result.
use_cases.notes: currentToolInvocationId is a Str::uuid7() per tool call, nested under the agent invocationId.

use_cases.name: Perform Similarity Search
use_cases.description: SimilaritySearch implements Tool. handle(Request) calls $using closure with request('query'), collects results, optionally reranks via Collection::rerank(), returns JSON string.
use_cases.notes: Factory: SimilaritySearch::usingModel(model, column, minSimilarity, limit, query). Schema exposes single 'query' string parameter.

use_cases.name: Feed Tool Result Back to Provider
use_cases.description: After executeTool() the string result is appended to the conversation as a tool_result message and the generate loop continues until finish_reason != tool_calls.
use_cases.notes: ToolCall data class: {id, name, arguments, resultId, reasoningId, reasoningSummary}. Stored on AgentResponse.

key_relationships: Gateway uses MapsTools (converts Tool/ProviderTool to API format)
Gateway uses InvokesTools (executes Tool.handle on tool_call request)
important_notes: Two categories of tools: (1) user-land Tool implementations (description/schema/handle lifecycle, executed inside Laravel by InvokesTools); (2) ProviderTool subclasses (WebSearch, WebFetch, FileSearch) executed natively by the AI provider — Gateway maps them to provider-specific type strings. MakeToolCommand scaffolds category-1 tools only. Tool schema uses Illuminate JsonSchema (Type objects). Tool name in provider API = PHP class_basename.
